---
title: "TORV Report"
output:
  tufte::tufte_html:
    css: theme/style.css
    self_contained: true
  word_document: default
  pdf_document:
    keep_tex: true
    latex_engine: lualatex
    includes:
      in_header: theme/template.tex
urlcolor: PineGreen
linkcolor: PineGreen
params:
  database_name: MASTER_DATABASE.xlsx
  html: true
---

```{r options, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = NA, dev = "ragg_png", dpi = 300)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE, results='hide'}
database_name <<- params$database_name
html <<- params$html
knitr::knit("templates/setup.Rmd")
```

<!-- report starts here -->

```{r logo-header, out.width="100%"}
knitr::include_graphics(here("report", "assets", "torv-logo-wide.png"))
```

```{r header-pdf, results='asis', include=!html}
cat(
  "\\bigskip  \\bigskip  \\begin{center}\\Large \\textbf{Report for ", 
  input_params$site_name, 
  "}\\par   \\large ", 
  format(Sys.time(), "%Y, %B %d"), 
  "\\par   \\large ", 
  "Eric Foerster\\end{center}",
  sep = ""
)
```

\newpage

## Executive Summary

```{r exec-summary-header, out.width="900px"}
header_file_create("None", "Executive Summary")
knitr::include_graphics(here(root_figure_location, "headers", "none_testing_executive_summary_plot.png"))
```

```{r exec-summary, results='asis'}
non_table_elements <- c("pH", "Sodium (ppm)", "Micronutrients", 
                        "Organic Matter (%)", "Total Nitrogen (ppm)")

txt_file <- paste0("exec-summary_", 
                   gsub(" ", "-", input_params$site_name),
                   "_", input_params$date_sample_submitted,
                   ".txt")

txt_path <- paste0(here(), "/report/", txt_file)

if (!file.exists(txt_path)) {
{    
  sink(txt_path)
  
  if(length(soil_types) > 0) {
    for(soil_type in soil_types) {
      
      cat("

### [", str_to_title(soil_type), "](#", str_to_lower(soil_type), "-1)", sep = "")
      
      cat("
      
* All calculations are based on a monthly maximum of", grass_max_n_per_month_per_1000sqft[[soil_type]], "lbs of N/M.", sep = " ")
      
      # Including the Nitrogen value regardless of flag status as the rest all depend on this
      cat("
* ", filter(measurement_commentaries,
              sample_description_number_1 == soil_type &
                measurement_name == "Total Nitrogen (ppm)") %>%
    pull(commentary),
  sep = "")
      
      # Adding commentary for the deficits tied to MLSN values
      filter(exec_summary_commentary, sample_description_number_1 == soil_type & 
               flag == "low" & 
               !measurement_name %in% non_table_elements) %>%
        pull(measurement_name) %>% 
        {
          if(length(.) > 1) {
            cat("
* Deficits were noted in the following ", length(.),
" measurements: ", paste(.[1:length(.)-1], collapse = ", "), " and ", .[length(.)],
". See the \"Required element per area\" table within the ", str_to_title(soil_type), 
" section for an indication of how much fertilizer should be added to each sampled area. **[TODO: Add custom comment.]**", sep = "")
          } else if (length(.) == 1){
            cat("
* Deficits were noted in the following measurement: ", .,
". See the \"Required element per area\" table within the ",  str_to_title(soil_type), 
" section for an indication of how much fertilizer should be added to each sampled area. **[TODO: Add custom comment.]**", sep = "")
          } else {
            cat("
* No deficits were noted in the values tied to MLSN values. **[TODO: Add custom comment.]**")
          }
        }
      
      filter(exec_summary_commentary, sample_description_number_1 == soil_type & 
               measurement_name %in% non_table_elements &
               measurement_name != "Total Nitrogen (ppm)") %>%
        pull(commentary) %>%
        {
          if(length(.) > 0) {
            cat("
* ", paste(., collapse = ". 
* "),
            sep = "")
          } else {
            cat("
* No other anomalies were highlighted in the analysis. **[TODO: Add custom comment.]**")
          }
        }
    }
  }
  
  
  if(length(om_types > 0)) {
    
    cat("
        
### [Organic Matter](#om-testing)
  ")
    
    cat("
The mean OM measurements can be summarised as follows: \n")
    
    for(unique_type_date in unique(all_om_data_comms$type_date)) {
      
      filter(all_om_data_comms, type_date == unique_type_date) %>%
        pull(type_date) %>%
        unique() %>%
        cat()
      
      filter(all_om_data_comms, type_date == unique_type_date) %>%
        pull(difference) %>%
        cat()
    }
    
    # If there is only one measurement, add in reasons why it's good to measure again
    if(length(unique(all_om_data$date_sample_submitted)) == 1) { 
     
      om_single_measure_commentary %>%
        cat("\n", ., sep = "\n")
  
    } else if(nrow(om_acc_df) == 0) {
      
      om_no_additional_analyses_commentary %>%
        cat("\n", ., sep = "\n")
    }
    
    if(nrow(om_acc_df) > 0) {
      
      cat("\n", acc_rate_commentary, "\n", sep = "")
      
      for(unique_soil_type in unique(om_acc_df$type)) {
        
        filter(om_acc_df, type == unique_soil_type) %>%
          pull(type) %>%
          unique() %>%
          cat("\n*", .)
        
         filter(om_acc_df, type == unique_soil_type) %>%
           pull(commentary) %>%
           cat()
        
      }
    }
    
     if(nrow(sand_req_df) > 0) {
      
      cat("\n", sand_req_commentary, "\n", sep = "")
       
       for(unique_soil_type in unique(sand_req_df$type)) {
        
        filter(sand_req_df, type == unique_soil_type) %>%
          pull(type) %>%
          unique() %>%
          cat("\n*", .)
        
         filter(sand_req_df, type == unique_soil_type) %>%
           pull(commentary) %>%
           cat()
      }
    }
  }
    
  
  if(length(water_types > 0)) {
    cat("
        
### [Water](#water-testing)

* **Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Maecenas facilisis sollicitudin magna at condimentum. Vestibulum at aliquam lorem. Aliquam ut nibh pretium, volutpat metus eu, fermentum velit.**")
  }
  sink()
}
} else { # if txt_file already exists just source it
  message(paste0('\n\n❗ An executive summary for this site already exists. Sourcing the file "', txt_file, '" in the "report" directory.',
                 '\n    - To add comments, open the txt file, modify its content, and generate the report again.',
                 '\n    - To create a new auto-generated executive summary for this site, please delete the txt file.\n'))
}

cat(readLines(txt_path), sep = "\n")
```

\newpage

## Turf Growth Potential Model

```{r interpret-results, eval=input_params$include_results_interpretation, child="templates/how_to_interpret_results.Rmd"}
```

```{r turf-growth-header, eval=length(soil_types) > 0, out.width="900px"}
header_file_create("Turf", "growth")
knitr::include_graphics(here(root_figure_location, "headers", "turf_testing_growth_plot.png"))
```

```{r turf-growth-rds, eval=length(soil_types) > 0, include=FALSE}
if (html) readRDS(here("report", "figures", "growth_potential_plot.Rds")) else knitr::include_graphics(here("report", "figures", "monthly_growth_potential_plot.png"))
```

```{r turf-growth-text, eval=(length(soil_types) > 0), results='asis'}
cat("The temperature data is provided by NOAA using 30-year climate normals and is site-specific to your location. Pace Turf, LLC ([Gelernter and Stowell, 2005](https://www.files.asianturfgrass.com/gelernter_stowell_2005_improved_overseeding_gp.pdf)) developed the growth potential model to explain the myriad of ways in which weather impacts turf growth.\n\nThe model considers <b style='color:#5b921e;'>turf growth to be good when the GP is between 50% and 100%</b> (the best possible growth occurs at a GP of 100%). However, when weather conditions are either too hot or too cold for optimal turf growth, <b style='color:#686617;'>the GP falls below 50%, and turf becomes progressively more stressed</b>. When <b style='color:#7c230d;'>the GP falls to 10% or lower, growth is extremely limited</b>. Appearing below is your model specific to ", input_params$site_name, ".\n\n", sep = "")
```

```{r turf-growth-interactive, fig.height=3.5, eval=length(soil_types) > 0}
if (html) girafe(ggobj = growth_potential_plot) else growth_potential_plot
```


## Soil Testing {#soil-testing}

```{r soil-results, results='asis', warning=FALSE}
if(length(soil_types > 0)) {
  
  map(soil_types, function(soil_type) {
    knitr::knit_expand("templates/soil_testing_table.Rmd",
                       sample_type = "Soil",
                       sample_description_number_1 = soil_type)
  }) %>%
    unlist() %>%
    knitr::knit_child(text = ., quiet = TRUE) %>%
    unlist() %>%
    cat(., sep = '\n')
} else {
  message("\n\n❗ Soil results not reported. Either no soil data or an error occured.\n")
}
```


## Total Organic Matter Testing {#om-testing}

```{r om-results, results='asis', warning=FALSE}
if(length(om_types > 0)) { # So the section only shows up if there are OM results to report
  
  map(om_types, function(om_type) {
    knitr::knit_expand("templates/om_testing.Rmd",
                       sample_type = "Soil",
                       sample_description_number_2 = om_type)
  }) %>%
    unlist() %>%
    knitr::knit_child(text = ., quiet = TRUE) %>%
    unlist() %>%
    cat(., sep = '\n')
} else {
  message("\n\n❗ OM results not reported. Either no OM data or an error occured.\n")
}
```


## Water Testing {#water-testing}

```{r water-results, results='asis', warning=FALSE}
if(length(water_types > 0)) { # So the section only shows up if there are Water results to report
  
  water_mod_full_database <<- full_database %>%
    mutate(water_sample_description = case_when(sample_description_number_1 == "IRRIGATION" & !is.na(sample_description_number_2) ~ 
                                                  paste(sample_description_number_1, 
                                                        "-", 
                                                        sample_description_number_2),
                                                TRUE ~ sample_description_number_1)) 
  
  water_mod_filtered_database <<- filtered_database %>%
    mutate(water_sample_description = case_when(sample_description_number_1 == "IRRIGATION" & !is.na(sample_description_number_2) ~ 
                                                  paste(sample_description_number_1, 
                                                        "-", 
                                                        sample_description_number_2),
                                                TRUE ~ sample_description_number_1))
  
  water_sample_descriptions <- water_mod_filtered_database %>%
    filter(sample_description_number_1 %in% water_types) %>%
    pull(water_sample_description) %>%
    unique()
  
  map(water_sample_descriptions, function(water_type) {
    knitr::knit_expand("templates/water_testing.Rmd",
                       sample_type = "Water",
                       water_description = water_type)
  }) %>%
    unlist() %>%
    knitr::knit_child(text = ., quiet = TRUE) %>%
    unlist() %>%
    cat(., sep = '\n')
} else {
  message("\n\n❗ Water results not reported. Either no water data or an error occured.\n")
}
```

\newpage

```{r, results='asis', include=!html}
cat("## How to Read Trend Line Plots {#how-to-read}")
```

```{r, include=!html}
header_file_create("None", "How to Read Trend Line Plots")
knitr::include_graphics(here(root_figure_location, "headers", "none_testing_how_to_read_trend_line_plots_plot.png"))
```

\par

\begin{multicols}{2}
```{r, results='asis', include=!html}
cat("The trend line plots show the raw sampling values (small grey dots) and the averages per sampling date (large colored points). The purple line indicates a linear fitting across all sampling dates This method is a general indicator of the trend of the average values across time. However, note that a linear fitting is not always suitable (i.e., be careful in case the distance between averages and the trendline is high). For reference, the grey dotted line indicates the historic average, estimated based on TODO.")
```
\columnbreak
```{r, out.width="300%", include=!html}
knitr::include_graphics(here(root_figure_location, "trendlines", "trend_plot_legend.png"))
```
\end{multicols}
